articles.title: Dollar plunges as strong shekel squeezes exporters: what can Bank of Israel do?
articles.expand: Dollar has fallen 25% against the shekel in two years, hitting exporters; Bank of Israel Governor Amir Yaron faces three options: buy dollars, sharply cut rates or do nothing — also a risk

articles.title: Qatar records over 73 million payment system transactions worth QR111.854bn
articles.expand: Doha, Qatar: The total payment system transactions reached 73.533 million in April 2026, representing a 29% increase compared to 56.869 million in April 2025. This growth surge in adoption of digital payment channels. The card payment systems continued to dominate transaction volume, accounting for 88.8% of total transactions, although this marks a slight decline from 94.9% the previous year. In contrast, transfer payment systems expanded their share to 11.2%, indicating growing reliance on account-to-account transfers and digital banking solutions, Qatar Central Bank (QCB) said recently on it...

articles.title: RBI Likely To Keep Interest Rates Unchanged In June Policy Review, SBI Projects FY27 GDP Growth At 6.6%
articles.expand: The RBI is expected to maintain current interest rates in its June monetary policy meeting, according to an SBI Research report. The report projects India's GDP growth at 6.6 percent for FY27, while warning that inflation, rupee weakness and geopolitical tensions remain key concerns.

articles.expand: While some central banks have hiked rates recently due to inflationary pressures triggered by the US-Iran conflict, others are deliberating a hike in the near future. Here is what could drive the RBI's response.

articles.title: 1.25 million young Britons could be jobless by 2030 as homelessness rises, government-commissioned report warns
articles.expand: Britain's youth face a grim reality of job scarcity and housing insecurity, with a 6% surge in homelessness and a projected 25% rise in 'Neet' individuals. Young people like Josh and Faye are sending thousands of applications for scarce entry-level jobs, often facing rejection and struggling to find affordable housing. Experts warn of worsening outcomes without urgent intervention.

articles.expand: The dominant story across financial markets last week was not artificial intelligence, central banks, or economic data. It was a dramatic shift in expectations regarding the future of the US-Iran conflict. Investors increasingly embraced the view that the three-month war is moving toward a diplomatic resolution after reports emerged that negotiators had drafted a 60-day […] The post US-Iran Endgame in Sight? At the time of writing, XAU/USD trades at $4,563, after bouncing off daily lows of $4,489.

articles.title: "Progress on lowering inflation has stalled": Fed's Bowman takes aim at Iran war
articles.expand: Federal Reserve (Fed) Governor Michelle Bowman said progress on lowering inflation has stalled while discussing the economic outlook and monetary policy in a speech at a conference in Iceland on Friday. Bowman claimed that the longer the Iran war continues, the greater the risks to inflation become.

articles.title: Canada GDP Contracts in March, But Early April Rebound Signals Resilience
articles.expand: Canada’s economy unexpectedly contracted by -0.1% mom in March, missing expectations for a 0.1% expansion and partially reversing February’s 0.2% gain. The weakness was concentrated in goods-producing industries, which fell -0.8% and recorded their fifth decline in the past six months. Lower activity in mining, quarrying, oil and gas extraction, as well as construction, accounted […] The post Canada GDP Contracts in March, But Early April Rebound Signals Resilience appeared first on ActionForex .

Decreased business and government capital investment was counterbalanced by higher household spending, as final domestic demand edged 0.1% lower. For that reason, there is still a chance that the pair turns lower again while the market trades below the 0.7278 invalidation level, which was the high from the be...

articles.title: German states see slight drop in inflation pressures in May
articles.expand: Here are all the state readings released around the same time: Bavaria CPI +2.6% vs +2.9% y/y prior Saxony CPI +2.7% vs +3.0% y/y prior North Rhine Westphalia CPI +2.4% vs +2.7% y/y prior Baden Wuerttemberg CPI +2.4% vs +2.6% y/y prior It's a bit of a change up compared to the preliminary readings for France and Spain earlier in the day. Even the monthly estimates here point to a marginal drop across the board. The breakdown shows monthly inflation falling in Bavaria (-0.2%), Saxony (-0.2%), North Rhine Westphalia (-0.2%), and Baden Wuerttemberg (-0.3%). Overall, that points to slightly softer...

articles.expand: Switzerland’s KOF Economic Barometer rose marginally from 97.8 to 98.0 in May, extending the modest improvement seen in the previous month. However, the leading indicator remains below its medium-term average, suggesting that the outlook for the Swiss economy continues to be subdued despite signs of stabilization. articles.title: French inflation continues to pick up in May, highest reading since February 2024
articles.expand: CPI +2.4% vs +2.5% y/y expected Prior +2.2% HICP +2.8% vs +2.9% y/y expected Prior +2.5% On the month itself, French headline inflation was up 0.1% in May compared to April. That reflects some moderation in energy prices over the past month but that is still much higher than what it was a year ago. For some context, the headline annual inflation rate is now the highest since February 2024. Looking at the breakdown, food prices held steady at 1.2% while services inflation ticked a little higher to 2.0% (previously 1.8%). The latter continues to be a key sticking point and with higher energy pri...

articles.title: France Q1 final GDP -0.1% vs 0.0% q/q prelim
articles.expand: Prior +0.2% French economic growth was revised down to -0.1% in the first quarter of the year, reflecting a contraction. That's not a good look especially as much of the drag came about from March, with the Middle East conflict weighing. As conditions look set to worsen further in the months ahead, that will make it very difficult to see an improved outlook for Europe's second largest economy ahead of the summer. As such, a technical recession beckons for France with stagflation worries surely set to rise as the weeks go by. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.

articles.title: Tokyo Inflation Cools Further, But Strong Growth Data Keeps BoJ Normalization on Track
articles.expand: Japan’s inflation picture softened further in May, but the broader economy continued showing surprising resilience. Tokyo CPI core (excluding fresh food), widely viewed as a leading indicator of nationwide inflation trends, slowed from 1.5% yoy to 1.3% yoy, below expectations and marking the sixth consecutive month of deceleration. articles.title: Tokyo core CPI misses forecasts in May, complicating case for BOJ June rate hike
articles.expand: Tokyo core CPI rose 1.3% y/y in May, missing the 1.5% forecast and extending a six-month slowdown, as fuel and education subsidies offset rising import costs, complicating the BOJ's case for a June rate hike. Summary: Tokyo core CPI, excluding fresh food, rose 1.3% y/y in May, below the 1.5% forecast and April's 1.5%, marking a sixth consecutive month of slowdown, per Japan Statistics Bureau Tokyo headline CPI came in at 1.4% y/y against an expected 1.6% and April's 1.5%, per Japan Statistics Bureau The core-core index, excluding fresh food and energy, rose 1.6% y/y, well below the 1.9% foreca...

articles.title: RBNZ's Silk flags near-term inflation pressure and rate hikes in coming meetings
articles.expand: RBNZ Assistant Governor Karen Silk said inflation pressures are building in the near term and the bias is toward rate increases at coming meetings, adding the bank can act without waiting for quarterly CPI data. Summary: RBNZ Assistant Governor Karen Silk said she did not think interest rates needed to increase yet but that near-term inflation pressures are building, per RBNZ remarks dated May 28 Silk said the RBNZ does not need to wait for a quarterly CPI print to move and needs to be more forward looking in its approach, per RBNZ remarks Silk said that even if the Middle East conflict ends s...

articles.title: Crude Oil futures settle up $0.22 at $88.90. Technical bias is to the downside.
articles.expand: Crude oil is closing modestly higher by $0.20 at $88.90, but the final price does not reflect the volatility seen throughout the trading day. Prices traded as high as $92.52 before reversing sharply lower and eventually finding support near session lows at $87.11. That wide trading range highlights the uncertainty currently driving the oil market from the Middle East war with Iran with the last news being in favor of a Memorandum of Understanding. That sent the prices lower. From a technical perspective, today’s low was important. The decline briefly pushed below the 50% midpoint of the rally ...
